text: Melee lane bullies are borderline unfunctional ATM with picks like Renekton and Aatrox getting absolutely shafted by Kayle and Shen. There's also a disgusting amount of range top laners, with Vayne being like a top 5 most played top laner. Voltaic and the Ambessa ult nerf just reduce the checkmate champions melee bruisers have against range top like Ambessa and Aatrox who relied on those items to turbo stomp the matchup. You have to build extremely gimmicky on a handful of melee champs to turbo stomp the lane, but only like Yorick can do that right now. Range top pigeonholes a lot of melee bruisers into being borderline unfightable and everything else scales too fast and lane holds too well like Kayle or tanks.
label: analysis
notes: BORDERLINE hot_take: references item nerfs + pick-rate claims (substantive) but uses emotional language and no hard numbers.

But realistically Morgana is getting blown up with an item that gives no defensive stats and her kit doesn't support her to stay in ult range.

text: Acknowledging you accidentally nerfed top Tahm and not walking back the changes, only to make him more frustrating as support, is so weird. Inb4 riot nerfs his q/passive again for being too strong in duo lanes, killing his top lane presence permanently. He was kept intentionally weak for over a year due to player frustration surveys (3 damage nerfs in a row). His early game is abysmal especially with homeguard changes, but his mid and late game feel good if you can get there with the introduction of DnD. He received a slight buff in his passive before his 1st skin in 1000 days, then is immediately hit with a double tap nerf on his passive ratios disguised as a buff. He hasn't cracked 50% in em+ in both roles in ages. Riot expressed that they want to support both roles but what they've done here is the opposite of that.
label: analysis
notes: Long, structured, evidence-backed balance argument with a proposed solution; some frustration.

Only downside is the Q CD change for base stats and a way better W for free pretty much (slightly higher E CD).

text: What I wish will happen but probably won't: Faelights removed or nerfed. Tower crystals nerfed (crystals + plates combo causes grubs to be omega weak compared to drake). Homeguards nerfed. Bounty system reworked again - if a bounty can go from 700g to 0 in a single play without that person dying then it shouldn't be 700g to begin with. A game state can be dead even but one team will have 2k gold total worth of bounties. Baron reward buffed. Marksman on-hit item buffs (bork, terminus, wits are all weak; dnd is strong but only a small portion of its users are actual on-hit champions). Timewarp tonic + vision runes in domination tree removed and replaced by actually meaningful runes.
label: analysis
notes: BORDERLINE hot_take: balance wishlist, but several points carry specific reasoning.

text: I think they need a full-on rework of the system, full stop, and I believe multiple devs agree. Melee mod Terminus so it fully stacks in 4 instead of 6 - the item should be built on Volibear, Yasuo, and Yone. Wit's End shouldn't be buffed, it needs to be reworked completely; it feels like an item only because of its legacy, and its passive of being the highest on-hit damage while statted as an anti-mage item is boring, plus it's an issue if the player also builds Terminus and Jak'Sho. Dusk and Dawn seems in a better spot when thought of as an AP Trinity Force; from what devs said it wasn't supposed to be an on-hit buy, it's that those who buy it have on-hit effects.
label: analysis
notes: Detailed multi-item rework reasoning with dev references.

text: Rengar Q AD Ratio: 100% - 115% => 110% - 225%. Huh. That seems like a lot there riot.

text: Undocumented bugfix: Elise Venomous Bite will now properly reveal the target during the cast. In 14.19 a QoL where Venomous Bite would reveal the target was given, but this wasn't properly functional until now. There are two ways of putting vision; they used the wrong method where Elise revealed a small static vision bubble at the cast location, meaning the reveal was pointless and players could dash out of sight cancelling the Q. Now the vision is attached to the target and follows them rather than being static at the cast location.
label: analysis
notes: Detailed technical explanation of a bugfix mechanic.

text: I really wish there were more ARAM Mayhem augment buffs instead of just nerfing what is good. A lot of augment choices have interesting designs but feel too underwhelming to justify taking them; Fetch, Gash, Mighty Shield, etc. Also, is Glass Cannon really only getting +3% damage dealt and no other changes? It's the worst augment in the mode by far. Compare it to Giant Slayer: Glass Cannon is +15% damage, -30% health, can't use BT shield; Giant Slayer is +10/15/25/30% damage based on size, smaller hitbox, +30% movement speed.
label: analysis
notes: BORDERLINE hot_take: data-heavy augment comparison, opens with a wish.

text: Unless you're a split pushing champ (Tryn) don't go TP top. Use ignite and snowball your lane. You aren't a pro nor in an elo where TP helps you secure drags or barons. Don't ever feel the need to help a teammate out of fear of being flamed - many times you go to help when you shouldn't because he's dead no matter what, and you end up giving the other team a double kill. Master 1-2 champs and don't fall into the meta hype. When you get a lead in your lane use it to make other lanes get a lead. Don't play a champ because a website says it's the counter - play what you're comfortable with.
label: analysis
notes: BORDERLINE hot_take: structured climbing/coaching advice with reasoning.

text: You say people in plat+ will never understand why people can't crawl out of bronze/silver. I'm sorry but that's such bullshit lol. It's so goddamn easy to carry at that elo, I can't even comprehend why people complain about elo hell. Once you actually get a single bit of skill you'll change your mind and carrying will become so easy. The amount of mistakes bronze 1/silver V players make is ridiculous.

text: After being hardstuck gold for the last 4 or 5 seasons, I have finally reached emerald. I'm aware I'm struggling to win now because my rank is accurate. I'm a top main transitioning to jungle (Yi, Kha'Zix, picking up Zac) because I wanted more direct impact. I think I'm reasonably good at CSing and warding and don't get terrible KDAs, but my ability to pick out fights and openings is terrible and I struggle to impact fights, usually dying before I can do anything. This leads to me dealing little damage even with good KDAs - sometimes as a jungler/toplaner I deal less damage than supports. I'm just looking to improve.

text: I literally do not understand the arguments against ranged top laners lmao, it's like saying there shouldn't be zoners in a fighting game or ANY fighting game. It's a stupid opinion and would make the game feel more stale.
label: hot_take
notes: Dismissive opinion with an analogy.

text: I am a top laner and tried to play both sides of the ranged top matchup. The problem isn't just how strong they are, it's how unfun and uninteractive it is for everyone, often including the ranged top players themselves. For the countered melee champ, the solution is usually 'sit back till lv 4/lv 6/a gank before you all-in', and the popular ranged tops build tanky, bring exhaust, have cc or escape specifically to stop you from doing anything, making the lane boring. If you mess up a trade or die, you get your wave frozen and can't do anything. For the ranged top players, these champs are hard to pilot; one mistake and they're a glorified ranged minion playing 4v5 while the enemy melee top becomes a raid boss. Even if the ranged top plays perfectly, is it fun to just cs and play keep-away for 15 min? The game plan is specifically to not interact with the other laner.
label: analysis
notes: Long, structured both-sides argument about why ranged top is unhealthy.

text: Been saying dorans shield and second wind had to be nerfed because of melee mids being unmoveable from lane, but people keep saying they need buffs - they can't unless you want mages suffering midlane, all it does is move the problem. My honest answer: give the toplane quest a buff where melees get hp regen when taking damage from ranged attacks, so you don't need to buff dorans/second wind and instead tweak the quest. Quests are the perfect thing to balance each lane in a way that doesn't affect other lanes - I'd do something similar with the botlane quest to help adcs against mages just for laning.
label: analysis
notes: Structured balance reasoning with a lane-specific proposal.

text: Hate to say it, but lowkey top laners brought the state of top laners upon themselves. They cried about jungle presence top lane to the point where Riot made it 100x harder to gank than bot lane - a single ward at 2:30 saves you from 9/10 early ganks. So now top has this 1v1 island while having less impact than a lane that's consistently 2v3/3v3 in high elo. It's not entirely the consequence of their complaints though; TP is pro-jailed way too hard imo and Doran's Shield/Second Wind could use a buff. Ranged tops benefit the most from all the changes since their major weaknesses (no TP for later, getting caught overextending) were addressed, and they accelerate their role quest faster.
label: analysis
notes: BORDERLINE hot_take: detailed historical/balance argument, opens with a bold claim.

text: Ranged tops show up as a symptom of something in the overall system being unbalanced. The actual reason is the durability patch changing breakpoints so ranged top laners need to make 2 mistakes before they die instead of 1 like it's supposed to be. This issue was bandaided by giving melees wind/dshield instead of actually admitting the durability patch was a mistake.
label: analysis
notes: Causal reasoning about the durability patch and breakpoints.

text: For real, it's the perfect storm this season of our tools against it being crippled and the rewards for snowballing being the most they've ever been. Until you get to Diamond they can't play ranged well enough to cause the carnage it is in high elo, so most people won't see an issue.
